summary: Backend (6/6 pass) and frontend (16/18 pass) tested for Akaal Integrated Solutions site. All required APIs work: GET /api/, POST /api/contact (with validation 422 for bad/missing email & short name), GET /api/contact (no _id leakage). Frontend hero, navbar smooth scroll, hero CTAs, all 4 service cards, tel: links across navbar/contact/footer, valid contact form submit (success toast + form clears) all working.

ui_bugs.issue: Empty-submit JS error toast ('Please fill in name, email...') never fires because all required fields use HTML5 `required` attribute — browser-native validation blocks submission first, so the JS guard at App.js L750-753 is unreachable. Functionally the user still gets validation, but the custom toast never appears.

action_items: Optional: To make the custom error toast trigger, remove `required` from name/email/message inputs (App.js L865, L879, L926) OR add `noValidate` on the <form> tag at L848. Otherwise current behavior is acceptable.

Mobile menu open-state was flaky in one Playwright run after viewport switch (close worked, open detection failed once). Likely a test timing issue, not a real bug — manual mobile testing recommended.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js is 1067 lines — consider splitting into per-section component files (Navbar.jsx, Hero.jsx, Services.jsx, Contact.jsx, Footer.jsx) for maintainability (>700 line guideline).
server.py POST /api/contact stores created_at as ISO string in Mongo but returns datetime object via response_model — minor inconsistency; consider storing as native datetime.
No rate limiting on POST /api/contact — public endpoint vulnerable to spam. Consider adding throttling (slowapi) before going live.
seed_data_creation: Created 2 TEST_-prefixed contact_inquiries via API during testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers all 6 critical paths. Frontend uses data-testid extensively (good coverage). Site loads at REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Contact form's custom JS toast for empty submit is dead-code due to HTML5 required attributes — not a real defect.
